title: momordica balsamina
intro: Momordica balsamina, also known as bitter apple or bitter calabash, is a plant species in the Cucurbitaceae family, native to tropical Asia and Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Momordica balsamina include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and aid in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being made into ointments and creams for topical application.
